revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
[[fr:Awashima-jinja]][[ja:淡嶋神社]][[ko:아와시마 신사]][[simple:Awashima Shrine]][[zh:淡嶋神社]] {{Otheruses2|{{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|qid=Q200747|lt=Wakayama City|1=Wakayama (city)}}’s Awashima Shrine|Other Awashima Shrines|Awashima Shrine|Awashima Shrine|qq=}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Awashima Shrine (淡嶋神社) |image = {{ill|File:Awashima-jinja, haiden.jpg|ja|File:Awashima-jinja, haiden.jpg|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Awashima-jinja, haiden.jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|270px
Haiden |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|qid=Q200747|lt=Wakayama City|1=Wakayama (city)}} Kada 116 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|16|25.3|N|135|3|59.4|E|display=inline,title|qq=}} |deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |type = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) Ronsya
Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} era |本殿 = Nagare-zukuri, cypress bark roofed |別名 = Kada Shrine |札所等 = | image = File:Awashima-jinja, haiden.jpg |festival = April 3 |神事 = Hina Matsuri (March 3) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=16|lat_sec=25.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=3|lon_sec=59.4|label=Awashima Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|qq=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|16|25.3|N|135|3|59.4|E|qq=}}|qq=}} }} [[File:Awashima-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right]] [[simple:Awashima Shrine]] '''Awashima Shrine''' (淡嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kada, {{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|qid=Q200747|lt=Wakayama City|1=Wakayama (city)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} with a former shrine Ranking of {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. It is the head shrine of all the Awashima, Awajinja, and Awashima shrines throughout Japan. Renowned for its doll memorial service, the entire precinct is lined with countless dolls—reportedly around 20,000—that have been dedicated from across the country. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}} (Sukuna-Hikona-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (Okinagatara-Shihime-no-Mikoto) However, various theories exist regarding the true identity of Awashima-no-Kami. == History == There are approximately 1,000 shrines in Japan associated with the Awashima Shrine lineage, but this shrine is recognized as the head shrine and boasts one of the most distinguished histories in {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. According to mythology, the enshrinement of the deities {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}} (credited with the creation of Japan) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} on the divine island of Awashima—one of the islands of Tomogashima off the coast of Kada—marks its origin. As recorded in the shrine legend, during the return voyage from the {{ill|Korean Conquest|ja|三韓出兵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Conquest|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三韓征伐|1=Three Han Campaign|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11249435}}, Empress {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} encountered a sudden storm in the Seto Inland Sea. While aboard her ship, she prayed, and a divine message instructed her to "throw the ship's thatched roof into the sea and let the current carry the vessel." Grateful for the safe arrival at Tomogashima, she dedicated the treasures brought back from Korea to the two deities. A few years later, when her grandson, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, visited Tomogashima on a hunting trip and learned of the event, he considered the island inconvenient. Consequently, he relocated the shrine to the opposite shore in Kada and constructed the present shrine buildings—thus beginning the history of Awashima Shrine. Awashima-no-Kami is also regarded as the consort of the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|qid=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} deities. A tradition tells that she was cast adrift to Awashima due to an affliction with women's diseases, vowing to cure them there. However, this account is generally seen as a later association arising from Awashima becoming part of the shrine lands of Sumiyoshi Taisha. As a result, Awashima Shrine is now a place where prayers for the healing of various female ailments—including safe childbirth and fertility—are offered (although at Kada Awashima Shrine, the healing aspect is attributed to {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}}, the deity of medicine). During the Edo period, devotees known as Awashima Gannin carried small shrines bearing the image of Awashima Myōjin and traveled around preaching the deity’s virtues, helping to spread the cult of Awashima nationwide. The current shrine building was destroyed in the {{ill|Kii Expedition|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Expedition|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10855721}} during {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s campaign, but was later rebuilt by Asano Yukinaga, repaired by Tokugawa Yorifusa (the first of the Tokugawa family of Kii), and then, in the late Edo period, constructed by the 10th generation Tokugawa Haruho. The present new shrine building was completed in 1979 (Showa 54). == Doll Memorial Service == {{ill|image:淡島神社 197.jpg|ja|image:淡島神社 197.jpg|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image:淡島神社 197.jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|thumb|180px|It is said that around 20,000 dedicated dolls fill the shrine grounds. Also famous as a shrine for doll memorial services, the precincts are filled with innumerable Japanese dolls and {{ill|Ichimatsu Dolls|ja|市松人形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimatsu Dolls|1=ichimatsu doll|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11480543}} arranged closely together for the memorial. In addition, local folk toys such as tanuki statues made of {{ill|Shigaraki ware|en|Shigaraki ware|qid=Q7496308|lt=Shigaraki ware|1=Shigaraki ware}} (including tanuki figurines), {{ill|Maneki-neko|en|Maneki-neko|qid=Q460679|lt=Beckoning Cat|1=Maneki-neko}}, and {{ill|Fukusuke|en|Fukusuke|qid=Q5507747|lt=Fukusuke Doll|1=Fukusuke}} are densely displayed. Some media have even portrayed this scene as a haunted spot. In response, the shrine priests have maintained that "allowing people to interact with and view the dolls constitutes a form of memorial service," and they value any interest in the dolls—a stance that has led to repeated clashes with the {{ill|Japanese Doll Association|ja|日本人形協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Doll Association|1=Japanese Doll Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28685743}}. In September 2016, several hundred dolls, which had been dedicated for the memorial service, were lent without the donors’ consent to an event at {{ill|Universal Studios Japan|en|Universal Studios Japan|qid=Q1375103|lt=Universal Studios Japan|1=Universal Studios Japan}} called “Halloween Horror Nights – The Curse of the Living Doll” (祟 TATARI ～生き人形の呪い～). This unauthorized lending of the dolls resulted in widespread public criticism. == Needle Memorial Service == Also renowned as a shrine for needle memorial services, a needle mound is constructed within the shrine grounds, where needles collected from across the country are memorialized every February. In recent years, as opportunities for sewing have diminished, the number of needles dedicated for memorial service has likewise decreased. == Prayers for Women’s Ailments == Based on the tradition that Awashima-no-Kami was cast adrift to Awashima due to an affliction with women’s diseases, the shrine is regarded as a place to pray for the healing of various female ailments, including those affecting women’s health, safe childbirth, and fertility. In the past, offerings for these prayers sometimes included phallic objects or even strands of one’s hair; nowadays, many women offer the underwear they once wore. In the innermost subsidiary shrine, numerous pieces of women’s underwear are dedicated alongside ema. == Festivals == * Needle Festival (Needle Memorial Service) (February 8) * Hina Matsuri (Doll Float Festival) (March 3) * Spring Grand Festival (April 3) * Tanabata Festival (July 1–7)[http://wakayama.keizai.biz/headline/180/ Awashima Shrine in Kada, Wakayama holds a "Tanabata Festival" with wish-filled strips] * Summer Festival (July 31–August 3) * Autumn Grand Festival (Amazake Festival) (October 3) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Bronze Sword with Rounded Scabbard, Unsigned (Craftsmanship) – A work from the Kamakura period. Designated on April 15, 1920 (Taishō 9){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|6315|Bronze Sword with Rounded Scabbard|qq=}}. * Large Circular Mountain-Shaped Star Helmet (Craftsmanship) – A work from the Kamakura period. Designated on April 30, 1921 (Taishō 10){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|5427|Large Circular Mountain-Shaped Star Helmet|qq=}}. == Gallery == image:淡島神社人形up 209.jpg|Dedicated Doll 1 image:淡島神社人形2万体 207.jpg|Dedicated Dolls 2 image:淡島神社人形・天狗の面 206.jpg|Not only dolls but various masks image:淡島神社人形・群れ 204.jpg|Corner of the Beckoning Cat image:淡島神社人形 203.jpg|Still beautiful Japanese Dolls == Access == The nearest station is on the {{ill|Kada Line|en|Kada Line|qid=Q76809|lt=Nankai Kada Line|1=Kada Line}} at {{ill|加太駅|en|加太駅|qid=Q11398584|lt=Kada Station|1=加太駅}} (Wakayama Prefecture). However, it is over a 20‐minute walk (some sites state 15 minutes, but that is incorrect), and only one taxi is usually available in front of the station. Public transportation is otherwise unavailable except for {{ill|Demand-responsive transport|en|Demand-responsive transport|qid=Q293849|lt=demand-response transportation|1=Demand-responsive transport}} (shared taxis)[http://www.city.wakayama.wakayama.jp/kurashi/douro_kouen_machi/1007740/1021406.html Wakayama City Demand-Response Shared Taxi] ([http://www.city.wakayama.wakayama.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/021/406/isshiki.pdf Kada area Awashima Shrine network]), so please be advised. == Surrounding Information == * {{ill|Kada Kasuga Shrine|ja|加太春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomogashima|en|Tomogashima|qid=Q7820112|lt=Tomogashima|1=Tomogashima}} * {{ill|Kada Awashima Onsen|ja|加太淡嶋温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada Awashima Onsen|1=Kada Awashima Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11398580}} == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Awashima Shrine|qq=}} {{osm box|n|1156133859|qq=}} * [http://www.kada.jp/awashima/ Awashima Shrine] – Official website * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0004990253_00000 Awashima Shrine’s Doll Float Festival] – NHK Video Archives {{神道 横|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|淡嶋神社|version=102103437|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:淡嶋神社]] {{Commons category|Awashima-jinja}} [[fr:Awashima-jinja]] [[ko:아와시마 신사]] [[zh:淡嶋神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Awashima Shrine (Wakayama) (Q7007362)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kii Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Nagusa district|ja|名草郡|zh|名草郡|qid=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}} || {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|lt=Awashima Shrine|ja|淡嶋神社|commons|Category:Awashima-jinja|fr|Awashima-jinja|ko|아와시마 신사|simple|Awashima Shrine|zh|淡嶋神社|qid=Q7007362|1=Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|34.273800|135.066672|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183145 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q7007362}}==Notes== [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:淡嶋神社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社]] [[Category:雛祭り]]
